sectionOrderRules: Hero must include service category, local context if known, and a primary booking CTA.
Service summary should highlight the main treatments or specialties without duplicating the full treatments page.
Visual proof such as gallery-preview or reviews may appear before trust-proof when data exists.
Trust-proof should focus on real signals such as hygienrutiner, certifieringar if provided, and kundomdömen without repeating the same claim across multiple sections.
The final conversion section should lead to booking or contact, not both equally.
Services intro must explain the salon's focus and who the treatments are for.
Service list items must be concrete and understandable, for example treatment type, result or session focus.
If pricing-highlights is shown here, it should remain summary-level and not replace the dedicated prices page.
Booking CTA should appear after the main service explanation.
Pricing intro should set expectations clearly, such as from-prices, package logic or consultation note when supplied.
Pricing table must use real service names and avoid vague placeholder labels.
Booking CTA should follow the price content so the visitor can act after comparing options.
Booking intro must explain how to book and what the preferred booking path is.
Booking CTA should be the dominant element on the page.
Contact alternative must provide a fallback path such as phone, email or contact form for visitors who cannot complete online booking.
Gallery should prioritize authentic treatment, result and environment imagery over generic stock-like repetition.
Images should support service understanding and brand feel, not overwhelm the booking CTA.
Booking CTA should appear after or alongside the gallery content.
About story must be personal and specific to the practitioner or salon.
Team should present real people or roles when available; if solo, it may render as a single practitioner profile.
Trust-proof should stay factual and only include credentials or routines that are actually provided.
Contact info must include the real contact methods provided by the business.
Opening hours should be shown when available and not invented.
Map should appear when an address exists.
Contact CTA may be a form or direct contact method, but should not hide phone or address when those exist.
